articles.title: Miss America 2025 Abbie Stockard Engaged to Lakers Center Walker Kessler
articles.original_title: Who is Abbie Stockard? The 2025 Miss America engaged to LA Lakers center Walker Kessler - Yahoo
articles.summary: Abbie Stockard, crowned Miss America 2025, announced her engagement to Los Angeles Lakers center Walker Kessler. The announcement was shared through a brief Yahoo article that highlighted the couple’s upcoming wedding plans.
key_points: Abbie Stockard holds the Miss America 2025 title.
She is engaged to Walker Kessler, the Lakers' starting center.
The engagement was reported by Yahoo with no additional details on timing or ceremony.

articles.title: Teen Finds Wilt Chamberlain’s Lakers Jacket at Goodwill for $4, Could Net $335K
articles.original_title: Teen snags Wilt Chamberlain's L.A. Lakers jacket at Goodwill for $4. He could make $335K - CBC
articles.summary: A teenager purchased a vintage Los Angeles Lakers jacket once owned by Wilt Chamberlain for just $4 at a Goodwill store. Experts estimate the jacket could be worth up to $335,000 if authenticated and sold at auction.
key_points: The jacket was bought for $4 at a Goodwill donation center.
It is believed to have been worn by Wilt Chamberlain during his Lakers tenure.
Appraisers project a potential resale value of roughly $335,000.

articles.title: Lakers Drop DeMar DeRozan from Trade Rumors Amid Offseason Reshuffle
articles.original_title: Lakers Reportedly Out of DeMar DeRozan Mix Amid Offseason Shakeup - Sports Illustrated
articles.summary: According to Sports Illustrated, the Los Angeles Lakers are no longer considering DeMar DeRozan as a trade target during the current offseason. The shift follows a broader roster re‑evaluation as the team explores other options.
key_points: Reports indicate the Lakers have removed DeRozan from their trade discussions.
The decision is part of a larger offseason roster overhaul.
No alternative trade partners or targets were disclosed in the report.

articles.title: Las Vegas Aces Rout Chicago Sky in Blowout Loss
articles.original_title: Sparks routed by Storm in another blowout loss
articles.summary: The Las Vegas Aces defeated the Chicago Sky 82‑64 after the Sky returned from an eight‑day break, failing to find solutions to their recent struggles. The Aces built a lead that peaked at 26 points, securing a comfortable victory.
key_points: Aces won 82‑64, extending the Sky’s losing streak.
Chicago briefly led early but fell behind by as many as 26 points.
The loss highlighted ongoing issues for the Sky despite the extended rest period.

articles.title: Lakers Unlikely to Sign DeMar DeRozan in Free Agency
articles.original_title: Lakers unlikely to sign DeMar DeRozan in free agency
articles.summary: Silver Screen and Roll reports that, despite frequent speculation linking DeMar DeRozan to Los Angeles, the Lakers are not expected to sign him in the upcoming free‑agency period. The article notes a history of rumors without concrete moves.
key_points: DeRozan has been a recurring free‑agency rumor for the Lakers.
Current indications suggest the Lakers will not pursue him.
The franchise continues to explore other roster options.

articles.title: Lakers earn win over Spurs in California Classic finale
articles.original_title: Lakers earn win over Spurs in California Classic finale
articles.summary: The Lakers closed the California Classic with a narrow victory over the Spurs, 88-84. Adou Thiero was a late scratch while Cameron Carr only played in the first half. Chris Mañon stepped into the starting lineup in place of Thiero, who was out with a wrist injury. The young Lakers had a rough start to […]

articles.original_title: Recap: Chris Mañon’s Dominant Two-Way Play Propels Lakers In California Classic Win Over Spurs
articles.summary: Following a sudden-death, double-overtime comeback win against the Miami Heat, the Los Angeles Lakers’ Summer League team looked to close out the California Classic with back-to-back wins, taking on the San Antonio Spurs at Chase Center.
